title: vigna minima f. heterophylla
intro: Vigna minima f. heterophylla, also known as the Vigna minima or rice bean, is a type of annual legume native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its small, hairy leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming Vigna minima f. heterophylla has been associated with various health benefits, including reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved blood sugar control, and enhanced cognitive function. Herbal preparations of Vigna minima f. heterophylla can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
